The classic projects feature may be disabled in some organizations, and GitHub recommends migrating to Projects V2 (accessible via GraphQL API) for new projects. Requirements: - The authenticated user must be an organization member with project creation permissions - Classic projects must be enabled for the organization - Requires 'repo' and 'admin:org' or 'write:org' scopes

nodes.label: Create an organization repository
nodes.description: Creates a new repository within a specified GitHub organization, with options for detailed configuration including visibility, features, merge strategies, initial commit, and templates.

nodes.label: Create an organization variable
nodes.description: Creates a new, uniquely named GitHub Actions variable for an organization, with configurable repository access visibility (all, private, or selected).

nodes.label: Create an organization webhook
nodes.description: Creates a webhook for a GitHub organization to deliver event notifications to a configured URL.

nodes.label: Create a project card
nodes.description: Creates a project card in a GitHub project column (classic projects only). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) was sunset on August 23, 2024. This API only works with existing classic project columns. New classic projects cannot be created. Consider using GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead. The request body must contain either: - `note`: A text note for the card - OR `content_id` AND `content_type`: To associate an issue or pull request

nodes.label: Create a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Creates a new column in a GitHub project (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

nodes.label: Create a pull request
nodes.description: Creates a pull request in a GitHub repository, requiring existing `base` and `head` branches; `title` or `issue` must be provided.

nodes.label: Create a reference
nodes.description: Creates a NEW Git reference (branch or tag) in a repository. IMPORTANT: This action ONLY creates NEW references - if the reference already exists, it will fail with a 422 'Reference already exists' error. To update an existing reference, use the 'Update a reference' (GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REFERENCE) action instead. The repository must not be empty prior to this operation.

nodes.label: Create a registration token for an organization
nodes.description: Generates a temporary (one-hour) registration token to add a new self-hosted runner to an organization for GitHub Actions.

nodes.label: Create a registration token for a repository
nodes.description: Generates a time-limited token required to register a new self-hosted runner with a specific repository.

nodes.label: Create a release
nodes.description: Creates a release in a GitHub repository for a specified tag; the tag must be unique for published releases, and if a `discussion_category_name` is given, it must already exist.

nodes.label: Create a remove token for an organization
nodes.description: Generates a token, valid for one hour, to authenticate removing a self-hosted runner from an organization.

nodes.label: Create a remove token for a repository
nodes.description: Generates a temporary (one-hour validity) token required to unregister and remove a self-hosted runner from a repository.

nodes.label: Create a reply for a review comment
nodes.description: Posts a reply to a specific review comment on a GitHub pull request, requiring the repository, pull request, and original comment to exist, and a non-empty reply body.

nodes.label: Create a repository dispatch event
nodes.description: Triggers a GitHub Actions workflow or a webhook on a repository by creating a repository dispatch event, allowing programmatic triggering of workflows based on events outside of GitHub.

nodes.label: Create a repository for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Creates a new repository for the authenticated user, optionally within an organization if `team_id` is specified.

nodes.label: Create a repository from an unpublished codespace
nodes.description: Publishes the specified codespace to a new repository, using the codespace's current state as the initial commit.

nodes.label: Create a repository project
nodes.description: Creates a new GitHub Projects V2 project board linked to a specified repository. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects V1 REST API has been sunset by GitHub. The project is owned by the repository owner (user or organization) and linked to the specified repository.

nodes.label: Create a repository ruleset
nodes.description: Creates a uniquely named ruleset for a repository, defining rules for branches or tags with specified enforcement, conditions, and bypass actors.

nodes.label: Create a repository using a template
nodes.description: Creates a new repository from an existing template repository; the authenticated user must have access to the template and, if creating in an organization, repository creation permissions within it.

nodes.label: Create a repository variable
nodes.description: Creates a new, unencrypted variable in a repository for GitHub Actions workflows; fails if a variable with the same name already exists.

nodes.label: Create a repository webhook
nodes.description: Creates a webhook for a specified repository; requires admin permissions on the repository.

nodes.label: Create a review comment for a pull request
nodes.description: Creates a review comment on a pull request's diff, targeting a specific line, range of lines, an entire file, or replying to an existing comment.

nodes.label: Create a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Creates a pull request review, allowing approval, change requests, or comments; `body` is required if `event` is `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `COMMENT`, and omitting `event` makes the review `PENDING`.

nodes.label: Create a scoped access token
nodes.description: Exchanges a user-to-server token for a new, fine-grained scoped access token for a GitHub App, requiring `client_id`, `access_token`, either `target` or `target_id`, and at least one permission; for repository-specific scoping, provide either `repositories` (names) or `repository_ids` (IDs), but not both.

nodes.label: Create a dependency snapshot
nodes.description: Creates a snapshot of a repository's dependencies to populate the GitHub dependency graph and enable security alerts; `sha` must be a 40-character commit ID, `ref` a fully qualified Git reference (e.g., `refs/heads/main`), and `scanned` an ISO 8601 timestamp.

nodes.label: Create a tag object
nodes.description: Creates an annotated Git tag object in a repository, pointing to an existing Git object (commit, tree, or blob) defined by its SHA and ensuring the `type` field correctly specifies the object's type.

nodes.label: Create a tag protection state for a repository
nodes.description: DEPRECATED: This API was sunset on August 30, 2024. Use GITHUB_CREATE_A_REPOSITORY_RULESET instead to create tag protection rules via repository rulesets.

nodes.label: Create a team
nodes.description: Creates a new team in an organization, optionally with maintainers, repositories, specific privacy, notification settings, or a parent team; if `parent_team_id` is given, `privacy` must be 'closed'.

nodes.label: Create a temporary private fork
nodes.description: Creates a temporary private fork of the specified repository to address a security vulnerability, linking the fork to a GHSA ID that must be specifically associated with this repository; the fork may take up to 5 minutes to become accessible.

nodes.label: Create a tree
nodes.description: Creates a new Git tree object in a repository, defining file/directory structure by specifying tree entries, optionally building on a `base_tree` SHA; all provided SHAs must be valid.

nodes.label: Create a user project
nodes.description: Creates a new GitHub Projects V2 project board for the authenticated user to organize and track issues, pull requests, and notes. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects V1 REST API has been sunset by GitHub.

nodes.label: Create a workflow dispatch event
nodes.description: Manually triggers a GitHub Actions workflow identified by `workflow_id` at a given `ref`, if the workflow is configured to accept `workflow_dispatch` events.

nodes.label: Create commit signature protection
nodes.description: Enables commit signature protection for a specified branch, requiring all new commits to be signed.

nodes.label: Create a custom deployment protection rule on an environment
nodes.description: Enables a custom deployment protection rule for an existing environment in a repository by linking a configured GitHub App (via `integration_id`) to control deployments.

nodes.label: Create draft item for user project
nodes.description: Creates a draft issue item in a user-owned GitHub ProjectsV2. Use when you need to add a new draft item to track ideas or tasks in a user's project.

nodes.label: Create inference chat completions
nodes.description: Tool to create chat completions using GitHub Models inference API. Use when you need to generate AI-powered text responses using models like GPT-4, Claude, or Llama through GitHub's inference endpoint.

nodes.label: Create inference embeddings
nodes.description: Tool to create inference embeddings using GitHub's AI models. Use when you need to convert text into vector representations for similarity search, clustering, or other ML tasks.

nodes.label: Create issue type
nodes.description: Create a new issue type for a GitHub organization. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization administrator. OAuth tokens need admin:org scope.

nodes.label: Create JIT runner config for repo
nodes.description: Generates a temporary Just-In-Time (JIT) configuration for a new self-hosted GitHub Actions runner for a repository; any specified non-default `runner_group_id` must be an existing runner group accessible by the repository.

nodes.label: Create or update an environment
nodes.description: Creates a new environment or updates an existing one in a GitHub repository, allowing configuration of deployment protection rules such as wait timers and reviewers; ensure `environment_name` is URL-encoded if it contains special characters.

nodes.label: Create or update an environment secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates an environment secret with an `encrypted_value` that was encrypted using the public key identified by `key_id` for the specified environment.

nodes.label: Create or update an organization secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates an organization secret for GitHub Actions, requiring its value to be pre-encrypted via LibSodium using the organization's public key.

nodes.label: Create or update a repository secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates a GitHub Actions secret within a specific repository; use `encrypted_value` and `key_id` to set or change its value.

nodes.label: Create or update a secret for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Creates or updates a Codespaces secret for the authenticated user; `encrypted_value` must be encrypted with the public key (ID: `key_id`) from GitHub's 'Get public key for the authenticated user' endpoint.

nodes.label: Create or update custom properties for an organization
nodes.description: Creates new custom property schemas or updates existing ones in bulk for a specified organization; each property definition must include `property_name` and `value_type`.

nodes.label: Create or update a custom property for an organization
nodes.description: Creates a new custom property (name must be unique for creation) or updates an existing one for an organization to define metadata for its repositories.

nodes.label: Create or update file contents
nodes.description: Creates a new file or updates an existing file in a GitHub repository; SHA can be provided to validate file updates, automatically fetched if not provided.

nodes.label: Create or Update GitHub Pages Site
nodes.description: Tool to enable or update GitHub Pages configuration for a repository. Use when setting up GitHub Pages for static site deployment or modifying Pages settings like custom domains and HTTPS enforcement.

nodes.label: Create or update repo secret with encrypted value
nodes.description: Creates or updates a Dependabot secret in a repository using an `encrypted_value` (pre-encrypted with LibSodium using the repository's Dependabot public key) and its corresponding `key_id`. This action allows you to securely manage secrets for a given repository, ensuring that sensitive data remains protected during the update process. By providing the encrypted value alongside the key ID, you can seamlessly maintain the necessary credentials for your repository's operations.

nodes.label: Create reaction for a commit comment
nodes.description: Creates an emoji reaction for a commit comment; if the user has already reacted with the same content, details of the existing reaction are returned.

nodes.label: Create reaction for an issue
nodes.description: Creates a reaction for a specified issue within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Create reaction for an issue comment
nodes.description: Creates a reaction for a specific comment on an issue within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Create reaction for a pull request review comment
nodes.description: Adds a specified reaction to a pull request review comment within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Create reaction for a release
nodes.description: Creates an emoji reaction for a specific, existing release in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Create reaction for a team discussion
nodes.description: Creates a reaction for a team discussion within a GitHub organization.

nodes.label: Create reaction for a team discussion comment
nodes.description: Adds a reaction to a team discussion comment, requiring the specified organization, team, discussion, and comment to exist.

nodes.label: Create sponsorship
nodes.description: Tool to start a new GitHub sponsorship or reactivate a past sponsorship using GraphQL. Use when you need to sponsor a maintainer through GitHub Sponsors with a specified amount and privacy level.

nodes.label: Create Sponsors Tier
nodes.description: Tool to create a new payment tier for your GitHub Sponsors profile using GraphQL. Use when you need to add a new sponsorship tier with a specific monthly price and description. The amount is specified in dollars and will be converted to cents automatically.

nodes.label: Create or Update Organization Secret with LibSodium
nodes.description: Creates or updates a Dependabot organization secret, requiring the secret value to be pre-encrypted with LibSodium using the organization's public key obtained from the 'Get an organization public key' endpoint.

nodes.label: Create user list
nodes.description: Tool to create a new user list on GitHub. Use when you need to organize and group GitHub users for easier tracking and management.

nodes.label: Create view for user project
nodes.description: Tool to create a new view in a user-owned GitHub project (Projects V2). Use when you need to add a customized view with specific layout, filters, and visible fields to organize project items. Note: This action works with GitHub Projects V2. The view can be configured with different layouts (table, board, roadmap) and optional filters. The visible_fields parameter is not applicable to roadmap layouts.

nodes.label: Decline a repository invitation
nodes.description: Declines a specific, pending repository invitation for the authenticated user, identified by its `invitation_id`.

nodes.label: Delete access restrictions
nodes.description: Removes all user, team, and app-based access restrictions from a protected branch.

nodes.label: Delete admin branch protection
nodes.description: Removes admin enforcement from a protected branch (branch name cannot contain wildcard characters) in a repository.

nodes.label: Delete a file
nodes.description: Deletes a file by path from a GitHub repository. The file's blob SHA is automatically fetched if not provided.

nodes.label: Delete a package version for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Deletes an existing package version associated with the authenticated user.

nodes.label: Delete an app authorization
nodes.description: Deletes an OAuth application grant for a user, which revokes ALL OAuth tokens and their associated authorizations for the application. This endpoint is only accessible to OAuth App owners and requires Basic Authentication using the OAuth App's client_id and client_secret.

nodes.label: Delete an app token
nodes.description: Revokes a single OAuth access token for an OAuth App or GitHub App with OAuth authorization. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires Basic Authentication using the OAuth App's client_id as username and client_secret as password. It CANNOT be called with personal access tokens, GitHub App installation tokens, or any other token type. You must be the OAuth App owner to use this endpoint.

nodes.label: Delete a reference
nodes.description: Deletes a Git reference from a repository; 'ref' must be fully qualified (e.g., 'refs/heads/branch' or 'refs/tags/tag').

nodes.label: Delete a release
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific release, its assets, and potentially its associated Git tag from a repository.

nodes.label: Delete a release asset
nodes.description: Deletes a specific release asset from a GitHub repository; this action is idempotent.

nodes.label: Delete a release reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a reaction from a GitHub release, provided the repository, release, and reaction exist.

nodes.label: Delete a repository
nodes.description: Permanently deletes the specified repository; this is a destructive, irreversible action that requires admin privileges for the repository.

nodes.label: Delete a repository invitation
nodes.description: Deletes an active repository invitation, permanently revoking a user's access to collaborate on the specified repository.

nodes.label: Delete a repository ruleset
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a repository ruleset.

nodes.label: Delete a repository secret
nodes.description: Deletes a named GitHub Actions secret from a specified repository; this operation is destructive and idempotent, and requires the repository to exist.

nodes.label: Delete a repository subscription
nodes.description: Deletes the authenticated user's subscription to a specified repository if it exists, effectively 'unwatching' it.

nodes.label: Delete a repository variable
nodes.description: Deletes a named variable (e.g., for GitHub Actions workflows) from a repository; the repository and the variable must already exist.

nodes.label: Delete a repository webhook
nodes.description: Deletes a specific webhook from a repository.

nodes.label: Delete a review comment for a pull request
nodes.description: Deletes a specific pull request review comment.

nodes.label: Delete an artifact
nodes.description: Deletes a GitHub artifact by its ID within a repository, typically resulting in an empty response (HTTP 204 No Content) on success.

nodes.label: Delete a secret for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Deletes an existing Codespaces secret for the authenticated user by `secret_name`. This is a destructive and irreversible operation. Returns 404 if the secret does not exist.

nodes.label: Delete a self hosted runner from an organization
nodes.description: Forces the removal of a self-hosted runner from a GitHub organization. This endpoint can be used to completely remove the runner when the machine you were using no longer exists. The runner will be immediately disconnected from GitHub Actions. Requires admin:org scope and organization admin access. Returns 204 No Content on successful deletion.

nodes.label: Delete a self hosted runner from a repository
nodes.description: Removes a specific self-hosted runner (by `runner_id`) from a repository, if registered there; this is idempotent.

nodes.label: Delete a tag protection state for a repository
nodes.description: DEPRECATED: This API was sunset on August 30, 2024. Use GITHUB_DELETE_A_REPOSITORY_RULESET instead to manage tag protection rules via repository rulesets.

nodes.label: Delete a team
nodes.description: Deletes a team (and any child teams) from an organization.

nodes.label: Delete a thread subscription
nodes.description: Call this to mute a specific notification thread by deleting the user's subscription; notifications may still occur if the user is @mentioned, comments, or due to repository watch settings.

nodes.label: Delete an autolink reference from a repository
nodes.description: Deletes a specific autolink reference (which automatically links external resource IDs like JIRA-123 to URLs) from the specified repository.

nodes.label: Delete a workflow run
nodes.description: Deletes a specific workflow run from a repository.

nodes.label: Delete branch protection
nodes.description: Removes all protection rules from a specific branch in a GitHub repository; the branch must currently have protection rules enabled.

nodes.label: Delete a code scanning analysis from a repository
nodes.description: Deletes a specific code scanning analysis by its ID from a repository; `confirm_delete` must be `true` if it's the last analysis of its type for a given tool and reference to prevent data loss.

nodes.label: Delete a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Deletes a specific codespace owned by the authenticated user; this is a destructive action and the codespace must exist.

nodes.label: Delete a commit comment
nodes.description: Deletes a specific commit comment, identified by its `comment_id`, from the specified repository; the comment must exist.

nodes.label: Delete a commit comment reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a reaction from a commit comment in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Delete commit signature protection
nodes.description: Disables GPG commit signature protection for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, meaning commits pushed to this branch no longer require GPG signing.

nodes.label: Delete a custom organization role
nodes.description: Deletes a custom organization role (which should not be actively assigned) by its ID; a 204 No Content response indicates success.

nodes.label: Delete dependabot secret by name
nodes.description: Deletes a specific Dependabot secret, identified by its name, from a given repository if both the repository and secret exist.

nodes.label: Delete a deploy key
nodes.description: Deletes a specific deploy key from a repository; to change a key's properties or access scope, the existing key must be deleted and a new one created.

nodes.label: Delete a deployment
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specified *inactive* deployment from a repository.

nodes.label: Delete a deployment branch policy
nodes.description: Deletes a specific deployment branch or tag policy, identified by its ID, from a given environment within a repository.

nodes.label: Delete a discussion
nodes.description: Deletes a specific team discussion, identified by its number, from an organization's team.

nodes.label: Delete a discussion comment
nodes.description: Deletes a specific comment from an existing team discussion within an organization, provided the specified organization, team, discussion, and comment all exist.

nodes.label: Delete authenticated user email address
nodes.description: Sends an empty request body to `DELETE /user/emails` to attempt deletion of user email addresses; the API typically requires specific emails, so outcome may vary.

nodes.label: Delete an environment
nodes.description: Deletes an existing deployment environment from an existing repository.

nodes.label: Delete an environment secret
nodes.description: Deletes an existing and accessible secret from a specified environment within a GitHub repository, returning an empty dictionary on success or error details otherwise.

nodes.label: Delete an environment variable
nodes.description: Deletes a named environment variable from a specified, existing environment within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Delete a gist
nodes.description: Permanently deletes an existing GitHub gist, specified by its `gist_id`; this action is destructive and cannot be undone.

nodes.label: Delete a gist comment
nodes.description: Deletes a specific comment from a GitHub Gist using its `gist_id` and `comment_id`.

nodes.label: Delete GitHub Actions cache by ID
nodes.description: Deletes a specific GitHub Actions cache from a repository using its unique `cache_id`.

nodes.label: Delete a GitHub Pages site
nodes.description: Deletes the GitHub Pages site for the specified repository; completes without error if no site is currently enabled.

nodes.label: Delete an issue comment
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific comment by its ID from an issue or pull request, if the repository exists and the comment ID is valid.

nodes.label: Delete an issue comment reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a reaction from an issue comment in a repository; the repository, comment, and reaction must exist.

nodes.label: Delete an issue reaction
nodes.description: Permanently removes a specific reaction from an issue in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Delete a label
nodes.description: Permanently removes an existing label from a repository.

nodes.label: Delete a milestone
nodes.description: Deletes the specified milestone if it exists; this operation is idempotent, typically returning a 404 if the milestone is not found or already deleted.

nodes.label: Delete an organization
nodes.description: Deletes a GitHub organization and its repositories; this is a destructive action and the organization name will be unavailable for reuse for approximately 90 days.

nodes.label: Delete an organization secret
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a secret from a GitHub organization, making it inaccessible to GitHub Actions workflows or other tools.

nodes.label: Delete an organization variable
nodes.description: Deletes a named GitHub Actions variable from a specified organization.

nodes.label: Delete an organization webhook
nodes.description: Deletes a specific webhook, identified by `hook_id`, from an existing organization.

nodes.label: Delete a codespace from the organization
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific codespace belonging to a member of the specified organization.

nodes.label: Delete a package for an organization
nodes.description: Deletes a specific package in an organization; cannot delete public packages with over 5,000 downloads.

nodes.label: Delete a package for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific package owned by the authenticated user; public packages downloaded over 5,000 times cannot be deleted via this API.

nodes.label: Delete package version
nodes.description: Tool to delete a package version using GitHub GraphQL API. Use when you need to remove a specific version of a package by its global node ID.

nodes.label: Delete package version for an organization
nodes.description: Deletes a specific package version within an organization; requires admin permissions for packages with over 5,000 downloads.

nodes.label: Delete package version for a user
nodes.description: Permanently and irreversibly deletes a specific version of a package owned by the specified user.

nodes.label: Delete a pending review for a pull request
nodes.description: Deletes a pending (unsubmitted) review from a pull request; this is only possible if the review has not yet been submitted.

nodes.label: Delete a project
nodes.description: Deletes the specified GitHub project (Projects V2) using the GraphQL API. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025 and is no longer available. The project is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. The user must have admin permissions for the project to delete it.

nodes.label: Delete a project card (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Deletes a project card from a GitHub 'Project (classic)'; this operation is idempotent. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action may return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

nodes.label: Delete a project field (column)
nodes.description: Deletes a project field (column) from a GitHub Projects V2 project using the GraphQL API. IMPORTANT: GitHub Classic Projects REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action now uses the GitHub Projects V2 GraphQL API with the deleteProjectV2Field mutation. Note: The built-in Status field cannot be deleted as it is a core field in GitHub Projects V2. Only custom fields can be deleted.

nodes.label: Delete project item for user
nodes.description: Tool to delete a project item for a user in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to remove an item from a user's project. Requires the project item's GraphQL node ID (starts with 'PVTI_'). Uses the GitHub GraphQL API deleteProjectV2Item mutation.

nodes.label: Delete a pull request comment reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a specific reaction from a pull request review comment, provided the comment and reaction exist on that comment within the specified repository.

nodes.label: Delete pull request review protection
nodes.description: Disables the requirement for pull request reviews before merging for a specific, existing branch in an existing repository; this action is idempotent and will succeed even if the protection is not currently enabled.

nodes.label: Delete repo codespace secret by name
nodes.description: Deletes a specific Codespace secret from a repository by its name; this action is idempotent and will succeed even if the secret doesn't exist.

nodes.label: Delete social accounts for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Deletes currently linked social media account URLs from the authenticated user's GitHub profile.

nodes.label: Delete team discussion comment reaction
nodes.description: Deletes a reaction from a team discussion comment, given the organization name, team slug, discussion number, comment number, and reaction ID.

nodes.label: Delete team discussion reaction
nodes.description: Permanently deletes a specific reaction from a team discussion within an organization.

nodes.label: Delete User List
nodes.description: Tool to delete a GitHub user list using GraphQL. Use when you need to remove a user list from a GitHub account.

nodes.label: Delete a package for a user
nodes.description: Deletes a package owned by the specified user, requiring admin permissions for the authenticated user; deletion of public packages with over 5,000 downloads may require GitHub support.

nodes.label: Delete workflow run logs
nodes.description: Deletes all logs for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository, provided the repository and run exist.

nodes.label: Disable a custom protection rule for an environment
nodes.description: Disables a specific, currently active custom deployment protection rule for an existing environment within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Disable a workflow
nodes.description: Disables a specified workflow (by ID or filename) in a given GitHub repository, preventing new automatic triggers; any in-progress runs will continue.

nodes.label: Disable private vulnerability reporting for a repository
nodes.description: Disables private vulnerability reporting for an existing GitHub repository, preventing direct private vulnerability reports to maintainers via GitHub's interface for this repository.

nodes.label: Disable Repository Actions In Org
nodes.description: Removes a repository from the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Only works when the organization's `enabled_repositories` policy is set to `selected`. Requires admin:org scope.

nodes.label: Dismiss a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Dismisses an APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED review on a pull request with a mandatory explanatory message. IMPORTANT: Only reviews in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state can be dismissed. Reviews in COMMENTED, PENDING, or already DISMISSED state will return a 422 error. To dismiss a review on a protected branch, you must be a repository admin or be authorized to dismiss pull request reviews.

nodes.label: Download an artifact
nodes.description: Downloads a GitHub Actions workflow artifact as a ZIP file. Returns the artifact as a downloadable file. Use GITHUB_LIST_ARTIFACTS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY or GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUN_ARTIFACTS to get valid artifact IDs first.

nodes.label: Download a repository tarball
nodes.description: Downloads a repository's source code as a tarball (.tar.gz) archive for a specific Git reference, if the repository is accessible.

nodes.label: Download a repository archive ZIP
nodes.description: Downloads a repository's source code as a ZIP archive for a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA). IMPORTANT SIZE LIMITATION: This action may fail with 'payload too large' errors for large repositories due to platform size restrictions. If you encounter size limit issues, consider these alternatives: - Use GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT to fetch specific files or directories (also lists directory contents) - Clone the repository using git if you need the full codebase Best suited for: small to medium repositories, downloading specific tagged releases, or quick code inspection.

nodes.label: Download job logs for a workflow run
nodes.description: Downloads logs for a specific job in a GitHub Actions workflow run, contingent on the repository's existence and the job ID being valid and having produced logs.

nodes.label: Download workflow run attempt logs
nodes.description: Downloads a ZIP archive of logs for a specific workflow run attempt. Returns a downloadable ZIP file containing logs for all jobs and steps in the workflow run.

nodes.label: Download workflow run logs
nodes.description: Downloads logs for a specific GitHub Actions workflow run as a ZIP archive containing log files for each job in the workflow.

nodes.label: Enable a workflow
nodes.description: Reactivates a currently disabled GitHub Actions workflow within a repository using its workflow ID or filename.

nodes.label: Enable GitHub actions in selected repositories
nodes.description: Sets the specific repositories that can use GitHub Actions within an organization by replacing the current list; only applies if the organization's Actions policy is 'selected repositories'.

nodes.label: Enable private vulnerability reporting for a repository
nodes.description: Enables private vulnerability reporting for a repository, allowing security researchers to privately submit vulnerability reports to maintainers.

nodes.label: Enable repo for Github Actions
nodes.description: Adds a repository to the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Requires the organization's 'enabled_repositories' policy to be set to 'selected'.

nodes.label: Encrypt and update dev secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates a repository's development environment secret using an `encrypted_value` and its corresponding `key_id`; the secret must be pre-encrypted with the repository's Codespaces public key.

nodes.label: Encrypt org dev env secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates an organization's GitHub Codespaces secret using an encrypted value and its corresponding public key ID.

nodes.label: Export a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Triggers an export of a user's specified codespace, automatically stopping it if active, and returns its export status and download URL.

nodes.label: Export an SBOM for a repository
nodes.description: Exports the software bill of materials (SBOM) in SPDX JSON format for a repository, if its dependency graph is enabled and it has at least one commit.

nodes.label: Find Pull Requests
nodes.description: Primary tool to find and search pull requests. Supports filtering by repository, author, state, labels, and merge status, and returns structured PR data for reliable use in workflows. GitHub search results are capped at ~1000 total items; narrow filters when totals approach this limit. `created_since`/`updated_since` filter on creation/update timestamps, not `merged_at`; apply merge-date filtering client-side.

nodes.label: Find Repositories
nodes.description: AI-optimized repository search with smart filtering by language, stars, topics, and ownership. Builds intelligent search queries and returns clean, actionable repository data. Check `incomplete_results` in the response — when true, results are non-exhaustive. Search endpoints are rate-limited to ~30 requests/minute (vs. 5000/hour for general REST); apply backoff on 403/429. Total results are capped at ~1000 per query regardless of pagination.

nodes.label: Follow Organization (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to follow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow an organization.

nodes.label: Follow a user
nodes.description: Allows the authenticated user to follow the GitHub user specified by `username`; this action is idempotent and the user cannot follow themselves.

nodes.label: Follow User (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to follow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow another user.

nodes.label: Force cancel a workflow run
nodes.description: Forcefully cancels a queued or in-progress GitHub Actions workflow run, bypassing conditions like always() that would otherwise continue execution. Only use when the standard cancel endpoint fails. Cannot cancel completed workflow runs. Requires write permissions to the repository.

nodes.label: Fork a gist
nodes.description: Forks a specified public gist, creating a copy under the authenticated user's account.

nodes.label: Generate release notes content for a release
nodes.description: Generates Markdown release notes content (listing changes, pull requests, and contributors) for a GitHub repository release, customizable via tags and a configuration file.

nodes.label: Get a blob
nodes.description: Retrieves the raw, typically Base64-encoded, content of a file (blob) from a GitHub repository using its SHA hash, if the repository and blob SHA exist.

nodes.label: Get a branch
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specified branch within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get access restrictions
nodes.description: Lists users, teams, and GitHub Apps with push access to a branch; this branch must be protected in repository settings for detailed restrictions, otherwise expect a 404 or empty response.

nodes.label: Get a commit
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific commit from a repository by its owner, name, and a valid commit reference (SHA, branch, or tag), supporting pagination for large diffs.

nodes.label: Get admin branch protection
nodes.description: Checks if repository administrators are subject to the branch protection rules on a specific branch.

nodes.label: Get all API versions
nodes.description: Retrieves all officially supported, date-based (e.g., "2022-11-28") versions of the GitHub REST API from the /versions endpoint.

nodes.label: Get all contributor commit activity
nodes.description: Retrieves commit activity (total commits, weekly additions/deletions/commits) for all contributors to a repository; may require a retry if GitHub returns 202 while preparing data.

nodes.label: Get all deployment protection rules for an environment
nodes.description: Lists all enabled custom deployment protection rules for a specific environment in a repository; the environment must exist and be configured for deployments.

nodes.label: Get a milestone
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific milestone within a GitHub repository by its number.

nodes.label: Get an app
nodes.description: Retrieves publicly available information for an existing GitHub App, identified by its unique URL-friendly `app_slug`.

nodes.label: Get an artifact
nodes.description: Gets a specific artifact for a repository by `artifact_id`. This action retrieves the details of a specific artifact from a GitHub repository using the artifact's unique identifier. It allows users to access information about artifacts stored in the repository.

nodes.label: Get an assignment
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub Classroom assignment if the authenticated user is an administrator of the classroom.

nodes.label: Get an autolink reference of a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific autolink reference (which automatically hyperlinks text like 'JIRA-123' to an external system) for a repository using its unique ID; requires administrator access to the repository.

nodes.label: Get an environment
nodes.description: Retrieves the details of a specific deployment environment for a given repository, including its name, configurations, and current status.

nodes.label: Get an environment public key
nodes.description: Retrieves the public key for a specified GitHub repository environment, used to encrypt secrets for GitHub Actions.

nodes.label: Get an environment secret
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata (name and timestamps) for a single secret in a GitHub Actions environment. Note: The actual secret value is never returned by this API for security reasons.

nodes.label: Get an environment variable
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific environment variable from a GitHub Actions environment by repository owner, repository name, environment name, and variable name.

nodes.label: Get an issue
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific issue in a repository using the owner, repository name, and issue number.

nodes.label: Get an issue comment
nodes.description: Action to get an issue comment.

nodes.label: Get an issue event
nodes.description: Fetches details of a specific issue event for a given repository and event ID.

nodes.label: Get an organization
nodes.description: Get an organization

nodes.label: Get an organization public key
nodes.description: Action for `GetAnOrganizationPublicKey`.

nodes.label: Get an organization role
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific GitHub organization role by its ID.

nodes.label: Get an organization secret
nodes.description: Gets an organization secret's metadata (e.g., name, creation/update dates, visibility), but not its encrypted value.

nodes.label: Get an organization variable
nodes.description: Retrieves details (name, value, visibility, timestamps) of a specific, existing variable for an existing GitHub organization.

nodes.label: Get an organization webhook
nodes.description: Retrieves the full configuration, including subscribed events and delivery settings, for an existing organization webhook.

nodes.label: Get a package for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific package (by type and name) from an organization, if both the package and organization exist.

nodes.label: Get a package for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata for a specific package owned by a GitHub user, using package type, name, and username as identifiers.

nodes.label: Get a package for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific package owned by the authenticated user.

nodes.label: Get a package version for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific version of a package within an organization.

nodes.label: Get a package version for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific public package version associated with a GitHub user.

nodes.label: Get authenticated user package version
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for an existing specific package version associated with the authenticated user, identified by its type, name, and version ID.

nodes.label: Get a project
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub project (V2) using its project number and owner. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 REST API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed.

nodes.label: Get a project card
nodes.description: Retrieves all details of a specific project card, given its unique `card_id`. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action only works with existing classic project cards that were created before the sunset date. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead.

nodes.label: Get a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific project column from GitHub Projects (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ Returns column details including: id, name, url, project_url, cards_url, node_id, created_at, and updated_at timestamps.

nodes.label: Get a pull request
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific pull request from a GitHub repository using its owner, repository name, and pull request number.

nodes.label: Get a reference
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific Git reference (e.g., a branch, tag, or fully qualified like 'heads/main') from a GitHub repository. The reference must exist in the repository; use 'list_matching_references' first if unsure what references are available.

nodes.label: Get a release
nodes.description: Gets a specific release from a GitHub repository, provided the repository is accessible and the release exists.

nodes.label: Get a release asset
nodes.description: Gets metadata for a specific release asset in a GitHub repository, including a `browser_download_url` for downloading the asset.

nodes.label: Get a release by tag name
nodes.description: Gets a release from a GitHub repository by its tag name; the repository and a release with this tag must already exist.

nodes.label: Get a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information about an existing and accessible GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get a repository public key
nodes.description: Gets a repository's public key for encrypting secrets to be used in GitHub Actions, if the repository exists and is accessible.

nodes.label: Get a repository readme
nodes.description: Fetches the README file (if it exists and is accessible) from a specified GitHub repository, returning its Base64-encoded content and metadata.

nodes.label: Get a repository readme for a directory
nodes.description: Retrieves the README file from a specified directory within a GitHub repository, optionally at a given commit, branch, or tag.

nodes.label: Get a repository ruleset
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific repository ruleset by its ID; if `includes_parents` is true, the search for this `ruleset_id` also extends to rulesets from parent organizations.

nodes.label: Get a repository rule suite
nodes.description: Gets detailed information for a specific repository rule suite by its ID, including its evaluation status and the results of its individual rules.

nodes.label: Get a repository secret
nodes.description: Gets metadata (name, creation/update timestamps) for an existing repository secret, excluding its encrypted value.

nodes.label: Get a repository subscription
nodes.description: Gets the authenticated user's subscription details for a repository, indicating if they receive notifications.

nodes.label: Get a repository variable
nodes.description: Gets a specific GitHub Actions variable by name from an accessible repository.

nodes.label: Get a repository webhook
nodes.description: Returns the configuration of an existing webhook for a given repository.

nodes.label: Get a review comment for a pull request
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific pull request review comment by its ID, provided the repository exists, is accessible, and the comment ID is valid.

nodes.label: Get a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific review for a pull request using its owner, repository, pull request number, and review ID.

nodes.label: Get a secret for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata (name, timestamps, visibility; not the value) for a specific, existing development environment secret associated with the authenticated user's GitHub Codespaces.

nodes.label: Get a self hosted runner for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific self-hosted runner registered within a GitHub organization.

nodes.label: Get a self hosted runner for a repository
nodes.description: Gets a specific self-hosted runner for a repository by its unique ID.

nodes.label: Get assignment grades
nodes.description: Retrieves all grades for an existing GitHub Classroom assignment.

nodes.label: Get a tag
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific Git tag object from a GitHub repository, using the SHA of the tag object itself.

nodes.label: Get a team by name
nodes.description: Retrieves a GitHub team by its slug from a specific organization.

nodes.label: Get a thread
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific GitHub notification thread using its unique `thread_id`.

nodes.label: Get a thread subscription for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Retrieves the authenticated user's subscription details for a specific notification thread, identified by `thread_id`.

nodes.label: List repository files and directories
nodes.description: Browse and list all files/directories in a GitHub repository. Efficiently retrieves the complete repository structure in a single API call when used with recursive mode. Perfect for analyzing codebase structure, finding specific files, or getting an overview of repository contents without cloning.

nodes.label: Get a user
nodes.description: Retrieves the public profile information for an existing GitHub user, specified by their username.

nodes.label: Get a webhook configuration for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves the configuration for a specific webhook associated with a GitHub organization.

nodes.label: Get a webhook configuration for a repository
nodes.description: Returns the configuration for an existing webhook on the specified repository.

nodes.label: Get a webhook delivery for an organization webhook
nodes.description: Returns detailed information for a specific delivery attempt of a webhook configured for the specified GitHub organization.

nodes.label: Get a workflow
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub Actions workflow in a repository, identified by either its numeric ID or its filename.

nodes.label: Get a workflow run
nodes.description: Gets a specific workflow run by its ID from a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get a workflow run attempt
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific attempt of a workflow run in a GitHub repository, including its status, conclusion, and timings.

nodes.label: Get billing usage report for user
nodes.description: Retrieves the billing usage report for a specified GitHub user. Use when you need to analyze billing costs and usage patterns.

nodes.label: Get branch protection
nodes.description: Retrieves branch protection settings for a specific, existing, and accessible branch in a GitHub repository; protection feature availability varies by GitHub product plan.

nodes.label: Get catalog models
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve the list of AI models available in the GitHub Models catalog. Use when you need to discover available models, their capabilities, token limits, and supported modalities.

nodes.label: Get a check run
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific check run within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get a check suite
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific check suite (a collection of check runs) by its ID from a repository accessible to the authenticated user.

nodes.label: Get a classroom
nodes.description: Retrieves details for a specific GitHub Classroom. Requires the authenticated user to be an administrator of the classroom.

nodes.label: Get a code of conduct
nodes.description: Retrieves the full details of a specific GitHub code of conduct using its unique key.

nodes.label: Get Code of Conduct (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to look up a code of conduct by its key using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to fetch code of conduct details as part of a GraphQL workflow.

nodes.label: Get a CodeQL database for a repository
nodes.description: Gets an existing CodeQL database (including a download URL) for a specified language in an accessible repository, if one has been successfully built for that language.

nodes.label: Get a code scanning alert
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific code scanning alert, which identifies potential code vulnerabilities or errors, by its number from the specified GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get a code scanning analysis for a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific code scanning analysis on an accessible repository, identified by its `analysis_id`.

nodes.label: Get a code scanning default setup configuration
nodes.description: Gets the default setup configuration for code scanning in a repository, including state, languages, query suite, and schedule for a repository if it exists.

nodes.label: Get a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Call to retrieve detailed information for a `codespace_name` belonging to the authenticated user, ensuring the codespace exists and is accessible.

nodes.label: Get commit authors
nodes.description: Fetches commit authors identified during a repository import, used to map authors from an external VCS to GitHub accounts. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

nodes.label: Get a commit comment
nodes.description: Retrieves the full details of a specific commit comment in a GitHub repository, using its unique identifier.

nodes.label: Get a commit object
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information (including author, committer, message, tree, parents, verification) for a specific commit in a GitHub repository, identified by its SHA.

nodes.label: Get commit signature protection
nodes.description: Gets the commit signature protection status for a branch in a repository.

nodes.label: Get commit statuses
nodes.description: List all statuses for a commit reference (SHA, branch, or tag) in reverse chronological order. Use when you need to check CI/CD status results from various systems for a specific commit.

nodes.label: Get community profile metrics
nodes.description: Retrieves a repository's community profile metrics, including health percentage and the presence of key community files (e.g., README, LICENSE).

nodes.label: Get contextual information for a user
nodes.description: Gets contextual hovercard information for a GitHub user; `subject_type` and `subject_id` can be jointly provided for more specific details.

nodes.label: Get a custom deployment protection rule
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific custom deployment protection rule (used by GitHub Apps for external validation or manual approval of deployments) for a given environment in a repository.

nodes.label: Get default attributes for a codespace
nodes.description: Get pre-flight data (e.g., default location, devcontainer path) for creating a Codespace in a given repository (must exist and be accessible), optionally for a specific Git ref.

nodes.label: Get default workflow permissions for an organization
nodes.description: Gets the default GITHUB_TOKEN workflow permissions and settings for a GitHub organization.

nodes.label: Get default workflow permissions for a repository
nodes.description: Gets the default workflow permissions (`read` or `write`) for the GITHUB_TOKEN and whether it can approve pull request reviews in an existing and accessible repository.

nodes.label: Get a diff of the dependencies between commits
nodes.description: Gets the dependency diff between two Git revisions in a repository, where 'basehead' specifies the revisions and 'name' can optionally scope to a specific manifest file.

nodes.label: Get a deploy key
nodes.description: Gets a specific deploy key, identified by its `key_id`, for the GitHub repository specified by `owner` and `repo`.

nodes.label: Get a deployment
nodes.description: Gets a specific deployment by ID from a repository, provided the repository and deployment ID exist.

nodes.label: Get a deployment branch policy
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific deployment branch policy for an environment in a repository, identified by its unique ID.

nodes.label: Get a deployment status
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific deployment status by its ID for a given deployment within a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get details about a codespace export
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific export of a codespace.

nodes.label: Get a discussion
nodes.description: Fetches a specific discussion by its number from a team within an organization. This is a read-only action that retrieves discussion details including title, body, author information, comment count, and reactions. The authenticated user must be a member of the organization to access team discussions.

nodes.label: Get a discussion comment
nodes.description: Fetches a specific comment from a team discussion within a specific organization.

nodes.label: Get emojis
nodes.description: Lists all emojis available for use on GitHub, including custom and Unicode emojis.

nodes.label: Get Enterprise Member Invitation
nodes.description: Tool to look up a pending enterprise unaffiliated member invitation by invitee and enterprise. Use when you need to check the status of a specific invitation.

nodes.label: Get feeds
nodes.description: Fetches a list of available GitHub feed URLs for the authenticated user.

nodes.label: Get a gist
nodes.description: Fetches a specific GitHub gist by its `gist_id`, returning comprehensive details if the gist exists.

nodes.label: Get a gist comment
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific Gist comment by its ID and the Gist's ID.

nodes.label: Get a gist revision
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific revision of a gist.

nodes.label: Get GitHub Actions cache usage for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves total GitHub Actions cache usage statistics for an organization, including active cache count and size across all repositories.

nodes.label: Get github actions cache usage for a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves the total count of active GitHub Actions caches and their combined size in bytes for a specified repository.

nodes.label: Get github actions permissions for an organization
nodes.description: Gets the GitHub Actions permissions for a specified organization, detailing repository enablement and allowed actions policies.

nodes.label: Get GitHub Actions permissions for a repository
nodes.description: Gets the GitHub Actions permissions policy for a repository, including its enabled status and the scope of allowed actions.

nodes.label: Get billing premium request usage
nodes.description: Tool to get billing premium request usage report for a GitHub user. Use when you need to retrieve usage data for premium features like Copilot or AI models.

nodes.label: Get billing usage summary for user
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve billing usage summary for a GitHub user account. Use when tracking usage costs, monitoring billing details, or generating usage reports for a specific user. Note: This endpoint only returns data for the past 24 months and is currently in public preview.

nodes.label: Get github meta information
nodes.description: Fetches GitHub's publicly available metadata, useful for configuring network security policies or IP allow-listing.

nodes.label: Get github pages build
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific GitHub Pages build for a repository, which must have GitHub Pages enabled.

nodes.label: Get a gitignore template
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific .gitignore template from GitHub by its name, which must be an existing template in GitHub's collection.

nodes.label: Get a global security advisory
nodes.description: Retrieve a global GitHub security advisory by its GHSA identifier. Use this to get detailed information about vulnerabilities including severity, affected packages, CVSS scores, and references.

nodes.label: Fetch GraphQL Node
nodes.description: Tool to fetch any GitHub object by its global node ID. Use when you have a node_id and need to retrieve the corresponding object details.

nodes.label: Get GraphQL rate limit
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve the authenticated client's GitHub GraphQL API rate limit information. Use when you need to check remaining quota, current usage, or reset time for GraphQL requests.

nodes.label: Get information about a sarif upload
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information, including processing status and results URL, about a specific SARIF (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format) upload for a repository, uniquely identified by its sarif_id.

nodes.label: Get interaction restrictions for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves interaction restrictions for an organization, showing which GitHub user types can interact with its public repositories and when restrictions expire; returns an empty response if no restrictions are set.

nodes.label: Get interaction restrictions for a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves active interaction restrictions for a repository, detailing which user groups are limited from activities like commenting or creating pull requests, and when these restrictions expire.

nodes.label: Get interaction restrictions for public repositories
nodes.description: Retrieves currently active interaction restrictions for the authenticated user's public repositories.

nodes.label: Get a label
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific label by its name from a specified GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get large files
nodes.description: Lists files larger than 100MB identified during a previous source import for the specified repository. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

nodes.label: Get latest pages build
nodes.description: Retrieves information about the most recent GitHub Pages build for a repository, which must exist, be accessible, have GitHub Pages enabled, and have at least one prior build.

nodes.label: Get a license
nodes.description: Call this action to retrieve comprehensive details for a specific software license recognized by GitHub, using its unique license key.

nodes.label: Get License (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to look up an open source license by its SPDX ID using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to fetch license details for compliance tracking or repository setup.

nodes.label: Get octocat
nodes.description: Fetches an ASCII art representation of GitHub's Octocat, suitable for text-based displays.

nodes.label: Customize OIDC subject claim template
nodes.description: Retrieves the OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim customization template for a repository, which defines the `sub` claim structure in OIDC tokens for GitHub Actions workflows; returns the default configuration if no customization is applied.

nodes.label: Get allowed actions and workflows for an org
nodes.description: Gets the list of allowed actions and reusable workflows for an organization when the 'allowed_actions' policy is set to 'selected'.

nodes.label: Get organization audit log
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve an organization's audit log events for security and compliance investigations. Use when tracking organizational changes, security audits, or compliance monitoring. Requires organization owner permissions and read:audit_log scope.

nodes.label: Get a custom property for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves the definition (schema) of a specific, existing custom property for an organization.

nodes.label: Get organization project item
nodes.description: Tool to get an item from an organization-owned GitHub Projects V2 project. Use when you need to retrieve details about a specific project item including its content, fields, timestamps, and creator information.

nodes.label: Get organization public key for Codespaces secrets
nodes.description: Retrieves an organization's public key, which must be used to encrypt secret values before they are configured for Codespaces.

nodes.label: Fetch Public Key For Secret Encryption
nodes.description: Retrieves the public key for an existing GitHub organization, required for encrypting Dependabot secrets. Requires admin:org scope.

nodes.label: Get org dev environment secret safely
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata for a specific secret available to an organization's GitHub Codespaces without exposing its encrypted value.

nodes.label: Get custom OIDC subject claim template
nodes.description: Retrieves the OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim customization template for a GitHub organization, which defines how the `sub` claim in OIDC tokens for workflows is constructed.

nodes.label: Get a dns health check for github pages
nodes.description: Retrieves the DNS health check status (e.g., CNAME/A records, HTTPS) for a GitHub Pages site; the check may be pending (HTTP 202) on initial calls or after site changes.

nodes.label: Get a GitHub Pages site
nodes.description: Retrieves information for a GitHub Pages site, which must be enabled for the repository.

nodes.label: Get page views
nodes.description: Retrieves page view statistics for a repository over the last 14 days, including total views, unique visitors, and a daily or weekly breakdown.

nodes.label: Get pending deployments for a workflow run
nodes.description: Retrieves pending deployment environments for a specific workflow run that are awaiting approval due to protection rules.

nodes.label: Get project permission for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves permission information for a GitHub project (classic) collaborator. Returns the permission level and full user object for a collaborator on a GitHub project (classic). The permission returned can be admin, write, read, or none.

nodes.label: Get public key for secret encryption
nodes.description: Retrieves a repository's public key for encrypting GitHub Codespaces secrets; requires `repo` scope or equivalent read access to codespaces secrets for private repositories.

nodes.label: Get public key for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Retrieves the authenticated user's public key for encrypting GitHub Codespaces secrets. The returned key must be used with libsodium sealed box encryption before creating or updating secrets via the API.

nodes.label: Get pull request review protection
nodes.description: Retrieves the pull request review protection settings for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, if such protection is configured.

nodes.label: Get rate limit status for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Retrieves the authenticated user's current GitHub API rate limit status, including usage and limits across different resource categories.

nodes.label: Get Raw Repository Content
nodes.description: Tool to fetch raw file content from a GitHub repository. Returns raw bytes in whatever format the file uses (JSON, CSV, binary, etc.) — parsing and validation are the caller's responsibility. Ensure owner, repo, ref, and path are consistent when combining with other GitHub tools to avoid mismatched-ref errors.

nodes.label: Get allowed actions and workflows for a repository
nodes.description: Gets the settings for allowed actions and reusable workflows in a repository. Requires the repository's `allowed_actions` policy to be set to `selected` (returns 409 if set to `all` or `local_only`).

nodes.label: Get repository development environment secret
nodes.description: Gets metadata (name, creation/update timestamps) for a specific, existing development environment secret (Codespaces secret) in a repository, without exposing its value.

nodes.label: Get repository attestations
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve attestations by subject digest from a GitHub repository. Use when you need to verify or check attestations for a specific artifact identified by its SHA256 digest.

nodes.label: Get repository clones
nodes.description: Retrieves the total number of clones and a breakdown of clone activity (daily or weekly) for a specified repository over the preceding 14 days.

nodes.label: Get repository content
nodes.description: Retrieves a file's Base64 encoded content or lists a directory's contents from a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get Repository via GraphQL
nodes.description: Tool to lookup a repository by owner and name using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need comprehensive repository information.

nodes.label: Get Repository Owner
nodes.description: Tool to lookup a repository owner (User or Organization) by login using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to determine whether a login is a user or organization and retrieve basic profile information.

nodes.label: Get repository permissions for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific user's permission level ('admin', 'write', 'read', or 'none') for a given repository, where 'maintain' role is reported as 'write' and 'triage' as 'read'.

nodes.label: Retrieve repo public key for encryption
nodes.description: Gets a repository's public key, used to encrypt secrets for Dependabot.

nodes.label: Get repository secret securely
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata for an existing Dependabot secret in a repository, without exposing its encrypted value.

nodes.label: Get repository security advisory
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve a repository security advisory using its GHSA identifier. Use when you need detailed information about a specific security vulnerability.

nodes.label: Get a delivery for a repository webhook
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific delivery for a repository webhook, identified by its `hook_id` and `delivery_id`.

nodes.label: Get rules for a branch
nodes.description: Retrieves all active rules for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, excluding rules in 'evaluate' or 'disabled' status.

nodes.label: Get Organization Dependabot Secret
nodes.description: Gets a single organization Dependabot secret's metadata (name, timestamps, visibility) without revealing its encrypted value. Requires admin:org scope.

nodes.label: Get status checks protection
nodes.description: Retrieves the status check protection settings for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, if status check protection is enabled for it.

nodes.label: Get team membership for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves a user's role and membership status within a specific team in an organization. Returns membership details including role ('member' or 'maintainer') and state ('active' or 'pending'). If the user is not a member of the team, returns is_member=False with a descriptive message. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization member with visibility into the team.

nodes.label: Get teams with access to the protected branch
nodes.description: Lists teams with explicit push access to a protected branch, provided team restrictions are configured in the branch's protection settings; returns an empty list otherwise.

nodes.label: Get the authenticated user
nodes.description: Gets the profile information for the currently authenticated GitHub user, including potentially private details based on user settings.

nodes.label: Get the combined status for a specific reference
nodes.description: Retrieves the aggregated commit status (e.g., success, failure, pending) from all checks for a specific reference (SHA, branch, or tag) in a GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get the hourly commit count for each day
nodes.description: Retrieves the 'punch card' data, showing hourly commit counts for each day of the week for an existing and accessible repository.

nodes.label: Get the last year of commit activity
nodes.description: Fetches weekly commit totals and daily commit counts for the last 52 weeks for a specified GitHub repository.

nodes.label: Get the latest release
nodes.description: Fetches the latest official (non-prerelease, non-draft) release for a GitHub repository; requires at least one such published release.

nodes.label: Get the license for a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves the license file and its details for a repository, optionally from a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA).

nodes.label: Get the review history for a workflow run
nodes.description: Retrieves the detailed approval history for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository, detailing each review's environment, state, reviewer, and comments, to track the approval process for workflows, particularly automated deployments.

nodes.label: Get the status of a GitHub Pages deployment
nodes.description: Retrieves the status of a specific GitHub Pages deployment for a repository, which must have GitHub Pages enabled.

nodes.label: Get the weekly commit activity
nodes.description: Fetches the weekly commit activity (additions and deletions per week) for a repository over the past year; best for repositories with under 10,000 commits.

nodes.label: Get the weekly commit count
nodes.description: Retrieves the weekly commit count for a repository, detailing commits by the owner and all contributors over the last 52 weeks; GitHub may return a 202 status or an empty response if statistics are being computed.

nodes.label: Get the Zen of GitHub
nodes.description: Retrieves a random quote from GitHub's 'Zen of GitHub' collection, reflecting GitHub's design philosophies and offering humorous insights, useful for displaying GitHub wisdom or a lighthearted message.

nodes.label: Get top referral paths
nodes.description: Fetches the top 10 most viewed content paths for a repository from the last 14 days.

nodes.label: Get top referral sources
nodes.description: Fetches the top 10 websites that referred traffic to a repository within the last 14 days.

nodes.label: Get user by ID
nodes.description: Retrieves a GitHub user's profile information by their unique numeric account ID. Use when you know the user's numeric ID rather than their username.

nodes.label: Get user project item
nodes.description: Tool to get an item from a user-owned project in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to retrieve details about a specific item (issue, pull request, or draft issue) in a user's project board.

nodes.label: Get user attestations by subject digest
nodes.description: Tool to get attestations by subject digest for a GitHub user. Use when you need to retrieve attestation data for a specific artifact or resource identified by its subject digest.

nodes.label: Get user's projects v2
nodes.description: Tool to list GitHub Projects v2 for a specified user. Use when you need to retrieve all projects owned by a user with pagination support.

nodes.label: Get users with access to the protected branch
nodes.description: Lists users with explicit push access to a protected branch, provided its protection rule restricts pushes to specific users.

nodes.label: Get Viewer via GraphQL
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve the authenticated user's profile information via GitHub GraphQL. Use when working with GraphQL queries or need viewer details in GraphQL format.

nodes.label: Get workflow external access level
nodes.description: Gets the access level settings for a private repository, determining how workflows outside this repository can use its actions and reusable workflows.

nodes.label: Get a job for a workflow run
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific job within a GitHub Actions workflow run, given its `job_id` which must be valid for the specified repository's workflow.

nodes.label: Get workflow run usage
nodes.description: Gets the billable time, in milliseconds, for a specific workflow run, detailing time spent on various operating systems.

nodes.label: Get workflow usage
nodes.description: Gets the billable time (in milliseconds, broken down by runner OS) for a specific workflow within a repository for the current billing cycle.

nodes.label: Check if repo starred by auth user
nodes.description: Use to determine if the authenticated user has starred a specific GitHub repository, which is confirmed by an HTTP 204 status (resulting in an empty dictionary in the response data); the action fails (e.g., HTTP 404) if the repository is not starred or does not exist.

nodes.label: List accepted assignments for an assignment
nodes.description: Lists accepted assignments (student repositories created after acceptance) for an existing GitHub Classroom assignment, identified by its unique `assignment_id`.

nodes.label: List repositories accessible via GitHub App installation
nodes.description: Lists repositories that the authenticated user can access through a specific GitHub App installation. Returns repositories where the user has explicit :read, :write, or :admin permission through the installation. The response includes permission details for each repository. Prerequisites: - Requires a GitHub App user access token (not a standard OAuth token) - Use GITHUB_LIST_APP_INSTALLATIONS first to get valid installation IDs

nodes.label: List app installations for user token
nodes.description: Lists GitHub App installations accessible to the authenticated user via their access token, including installation details, permissions, and repository access.

nodes.label: Get apps with access to the protected branch
nodes.description: Lists GitHub Apps with push access to a repository's protected branch.

nodes.label: List artifacts for a repository
nodes.description: Lists GitHub Actions workflow artifacts for a specified repository, which must exist.

nodes.label: List assignees
nodes.description: Lists users who can be assigned to issues in a repository, typically those with push access.

nodes.label: List assignments for a classroom
nodes.description: Lists all assignments for a given GitHub Classroom `classroom_id`; the classroom must exist and be accessible.

nodes.label: List attestation repositories
